projects.title: Featured projects
projects.subtitle: A few products we've shipped for clients at home and abroad.

mit.desc: Corporate website for Mijuit IT, a Korean enterprise software firm. Clean Angular-driven marketing layout with smooth routing and a responsive multi-device experience.

order.desc: Large-scale sales order & ERP platform used by Mijuit's distribution team. Reactive Forms, live inventory sync, multi-role access, Chart.js dashboards, and printable invoices.

mood.desc: Admin dashboard and storefront for a Vietnamese bubble-tea brand. Full CRUD product catalog, real-time stock, filtered order history with pagination.

chamwoodon.desc: E-commerce site for a Korean wood-aged spirits brand. Reusable UI, Angular Router-driven flows, and a checkout that ships in three steps.

datsan.desc: Booking platform for a Vietnamese sports venue. Real-time slot availability, multi-role access, and admin dashboards built on Next.js + TypeScript.

hanroyal.desc: E-commerce site for a Korean premium meat brand. Reusable UI, Angular Router-driven flows, and a checkout that ships in three steps.

tech.subtitle: A modern toolkit to ship products fast and built to last.

process.title: My work process
process.subtitle: A transparent 5-step process — you always know where the project stands.

one.title: Discovery call
one.desc: A 30-minute call to understand goals, scope and expectations.

two.title: Proposal & contract
two.desc: Detailed solution proposal with clear timeline and budget.

three.title: UI/UX design
three.desc: Wireframes, mockups and interactive prototypes before any code.

four.title: Development
four.desc: 1–2 week sprints with regular demos so you can track progress.

five.title: Launch & support
five.desc: Deployment, training and 30 days of post-launch technical support.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.subtitle: Still have questions? Get in touch directly.

one.a: Absolutely. Every stage (wireframe, mockup, prototype) has a review round and you can request changes until you're happy.

two.q: Who owns the source code after delivery?
two.a: You do — 100%. We'll transfer the entire repository, hosting accounts and technical documentation to you.

three.a: Yes. Every package includes 30 days of free bug-fix support. After that, monthly maintenance plans are available.

four.q: Can I hire the team hourly?
four.a: Yes, at $35–60/hour depending on complexity and role. However, fixed-scope projects usually work out cheaper for you.

five.a: Happy to. We'll sign an NDA before discussing project details if you request one.

payment.title: Payment terms
payment.subtitle: Simple, fair and transparent for both sides.

upfront.title: 50% upfront
upfront.desc: After contract signing, to kick off design and development.

delivery.title: 50% on delivery
delivery.desc: Before production deployment and source code handover.

one.q: Can I get a refund if I'm not satisfied?
one.a: Yes. In the first 7 days you can request a refund of the upfront payment (minus work already completed).

two.a: Each package specifies a number of revision rounds. Additional rounds are $80–150 each depending on scope.

three.a: For projects over $5,000 we can break payment into 3–4 milestones rather than just 50/50.

four.q: What if the project runs over schedule?
four.a: If it's on the team: we keep working at no extra cost. If scope changes on your side: we'll handle it via a change request and additional quote.

five.a: Yes — VAT invoices via a partner company for Vietnam, international invoices for overseas clients.

six.a: No. Hosting, domain and third-party services (Stripe, Firebase…) are paid by you based on actual usage.

hero.title: A small studio focused on high-quality digital products
hero.tagline: Specialist team · Globally remote · Devoted to every line of code
hero.bio: We're a tight-knit team of developers, designers and product specialists — united by a passion for shipping beautiful, fast and reliable digital products for businesses of any size.

story.title: Our journey
paragraphs: Navigin was founded in 2025 by a small group of developers and designers who'd worked together at tech companies and agencies in Vietnam and Korea. After years of shipping products for others, we decided to start a studio where quality comes before quantity.

Today the team is intentionally small — every project gets the senior attention it deserves. So far we've shipped six products in production for clients in Vietnam and Korea, from corporate websites and ERP platforms to e-commerce and booking apps.
What we love most is partnering with founders and teams who have vision — people who dare to turn ideas into action. Our joy is shaping those ideas into shipped products that drive real value.
